title: Git init, .gitignore, create private GitHub repo
description: Initialize git repository, create .gitignore for C++/macOS, create private repo on GitHub, push initial commit.

title: Update README with roadmap and architecture
description: Expand README with architecture diagram, roadmap phases, MCP tools table, build instructions, and installation guide.

title: Create issues.jsonl tracking system
description: Create issues.jsonl file to track all bugs, features, plans, and changes. Establish tracking conventions.

title: Scaffold project structure
description: Create Makefile, src/ directory, download nlohmann/json header, create source files (main.cpp, mcp_handler.h/cpp, midi_bridge.h/cpp).

title: Implement JSON-RPC/MCP protocol handler
description: Implement MCP protocol over stdio: initialize handshake, tools/list, tools/call dispatch. JSON-RPC 2.0 request parsing and response formatting.

title: Implement CoreMIDI integration
description: Wrap CoreMIDI API: enumerate sources/destinations, send messages, receive with FIFO buffering, port open/close lifecycle.

title: Wire up main entry point and build
description: Connect MCP handler and MIDI bridge in main.cpp. Stdio read loop, signal handling, clean shutdown. Verified build and smoke test.

title: SDK include path workaround for broken CommandLineTools
description: C++ stdlib headers missing from CommandLineTools include dir. Fixed by adding -isysroot and explicit SDK include path in Makefile.

title: Live test MCP with Claude Code
description: Restart Claude Code to load apple-midi MCP server. Test list_devices, send_message, open_port, read_buffer, close_port live. First priority next session.

title: Test send_message with real hardware
description: Send note on/off, CC, program change to MIDI 16x16 destinations. Verify messages arrive on connected instruments. Tested: kick, snare, hi-hat on port 10 drum machine.

title: Test receive MIDI with FIFO buffering
description: Open port on MIDI 16x16 source, play notes on connected controller, read_buffer to verify incoming data. Validate FIFO flush behavior. Verified: notes, CC, aftertouch, pitch bend all received correctly on port 3 (Blofeld).

title: Error handling and input validation
description: Validate tool arguments (byte ranges 0-255, valid device IDs, required params). Return clear error messages for invalid input. 22 protocol tests passing.

title: SysEx support with safety guardrails
description: SysEx send and receive verified end-to-end. Loopback test (port 8): 6-byte and 391-byte SysEx both roundtrip perfectly, multi-packet reassembly works (133 packets). Real hardware test (Waldorf Pulse 2, port 12): identity request and sound dump request both return correct responses matching midi-console byte-for-byte. Blofeld (port 3) does not respond to SysEx — confirmed as device-side config issue via independent midi-console testing.

title: Virtual MIDI port creation
description: Allow creating virtual MIDI ports so other apps can send/receive through the MCP server.

title: Device hot-plug detection
description: Detect MIDI device connect/disconnect events and notify the client or update device list.

title: SMF (Standard MIDI File) playback
description: Parse .mid files and play them back with proper timing to a destination port. New MCP tool play_file that takes a file path, destination ID, and optional tempo override. Handles multi-track SMFs, delta timing, tempo maps. Enables Claude to play back full MIDI sequences with accurate timing rather than individual note-by-note sends.

title: Extract reusable McpServer class from McpHandler
description: Refactor McpHandler into a generic McpServer class (matching screen-capture-mcp and video-capture-mcp pattern) that owns the stdio loop, message framing, JSON-RPC dispatch, and tool registration via add_tool(). MIDI-specific tool implementations move to main.cpp or a separate file. This separates protocol plumbing from domain logic, making the MCP layer reusable and consistent across projects.

title: Add Content-Length framing support
description: Support both NDJSON and Content-Length (LSP-style) message framing, auto-detected from the first message. Currently only NDJSON is supported. Some MCP clients (e.g. Claude Desktop) may use Content-Length framing. Match the read_message/write_message pattern from screen-capture-mcp and video-capture-mcp.

title: Migrate build system from Makefile to CMake
description: Replace hand-rolled Makefile with CMakeLists.txt using CMake 3.20+, C++17, FetchContent for nlohmann/json (removing vendored deps/json.hpp). Add a thin Makefile wrapper (build/clean/rebuild targets) for convenience. Matches the build pattern used by screen-capture-mcp and video-capture-mcp, and improves portability.

title: Add tool exception safety to tools/call dispatch
description: If a tool handler throws an unexpected exception, the server currently crashes or returns an unstructured error. Wrap tool dispatch in try/catch and return a proper MCP error response with isError:true, matching the pattern in the McpServer class from screen-capture-mcp. This makes the server resilient to unexpected failures in tool implementations.

title: Hardcode protocol version instead of echoing client version
description: The initialize handler currently echoes back whatever protocolVersion the client sends, which is incorrect — the server should declare the version it actually implements (2024-11-05). If the client requests a version the server doesn't support, it should still return its own version so the client can decide whether to proceed. Both screen-capture-mcp and video-capture-mcp hardcode their version.
